chunks.summary: Decided to keep `.github` in the repo. Discussed workflows for CI (format validation, markdown lint) and auto releases. Templates for issues and PRs noted as essential.
Decided to keep `.github` to maintain CI badge in README and skill format validation on PRs for spellbook functionality.
Spellbook can't be used in Codex as it relies on Claude Code's native features (skills, commands, agents, hooks). Codex uses AGENTS.md instead of CLAUDE.md.
Last chat: Image input methods, README fixes (CI badge, sub-headline), logo added, multiFileRAG agents/skills installed, Azure skill created.
Codex can't use Claude Code skills, hooks, commands, or agents. No code changes or decisions made. Discussed limitations of Codex vs. Claude Code features.
Created `ai-engineer` skill: added to CLAUDE.md & README.md. Updated skill file with model selection, RAG arch, agent orchestration, prompt engineering. Bumped README badge count to 25.

Decisions: Implement RAG pattern for complex docs (Excel, CSV, PDFs). Code changes: Added `skills/complex-doc-rag/skill.md`. Topics: edge cases, extraction tools, processing strategy. Problems solved: structured handling of diverse document types.
Decided to create a new repo named `codex-spellbook`. Prompt provided for repo structure: AGENTS.md, task prompts, agent templates for Codex.
Decided to focus on the "spellbook" concept for LinkedIn post. Code changes not discussed. Topics: repo announcement, AI coding assistants, structured knowledge libraries.
Dev AI tool `claude-spellbook` created for coding assistance. Encodes standards as markdown "spells" for context. Enhances dev lifecycle efficiency.
Decided to create `claude-spellbook` lib for reusability. Focused on cursor-based pagination, table splitting, and Excel to DataFrame conversion. Enhanced session efficiency.
Built library for auto-loading domain knowledge (API, RAG, security, tests). 26 skills, 5 agents, 12 commands, MCP srv for persistent memory. Focus on RAG skill for real-world docs.

Dev: Built RAG skill for PDFs, Excel, images. Created library for auto-loading domain knowledge (26 skills, 5 agents, 12 cmds). MCP srv for persistent memory. Discussed API design, security, test gen.
MCP srv not connected; `load_memory`/`load_history` tools unavailable. Config check needed. History hooks active; `.mcp_history.json` has data since Apr 16.
Memory_map code present; venv missing. Decision: create venv & install deps. Code changes: cmd to create venv & install from requirements.txt. Next step: re-register MCP srv.
Discussed `/mem_save` functionality; confirmed it uses hooks via `history_hook.py` with system Python (no venv). MCP server down affects `save_history`. Global deps: `httpx`, `dotenv`.
Discussed MCP vs. memory_map: MCP enables read/write, hooks are write-only. MCP reads history for Claude; hooks save summaries. No code changes made.
Fix in mem_map proj confirmed. Steps: 1) Create venv & install deps. 2) Register MCP srv with Claude. Restart session after changes.
Fixed `C:\Users\Sidhartha\memory_map`. Verified venv & packages, registered MCP srv. Update CLAUDE.md to point to correct `server.py` path. Tools ready: `load_memory`, `load_history`, `save_memory`.
Change README for MCP reg to `--scope user`. Update hook setup to global for all projects. Store in `~/.claude.json`.
